name: academic-research-suite-ads

source_repositories.name: academic-research-skills
included_paths: top-level README, README.zh-TW, README.zh-CN, README.ja-JP, README.ko-KR, QUICKSTART, SECURITY, NOTICE, CONTRIBUTING, MODE_REGISTRY, POSITIONING, CITATION.cff, THIRD_PARTY.md

inactive_upstream_scripts.reason: Requires upstream .claude/CLAUDE.md suite-version table, which is intentionally not vendored in the Codex package.
Claude Code plugin hook metadata. Preserved for upstream traceability but not installed or executed by Codex.

historical_artifacts.disclaimer: Upstream design/plan document, vendored for traceability. NOT a Codex runtime instruction. References to superpowers:* skills, maintainer-local paths, or Claude Code session describe the upstream development environment.

Upstream design/plan document, vendored for traceability. NOT a Codex runtime instruction. References to superpowers:* skills, maintainer-local paths, or Claude Code session describe the upstream development environment.

runtime_notes: This ADS edition overlays the pinned ARS v3.18.0 source with SAO/NASA ADS discovery and a bibcode-gated fifth citation-existence resolver; the upstream source commit remains unchanged.
Claude Code agent-team wording is adapted by SKILL.md into Codex inline role-prompt usage.
The Codex-only codex/ adapter profile is optional and disabled by default; it provides planner metadata, agent-team templates, hook metadata, and quality gates for explicit full-runtime use.
The maintainer's local ARS checkout was not used as a write target.
Upstream slash commands are vendored as prompt recipes and emulated through the Codex SKILL.md alias router; Codex does not register native slash commands.
Vendored upstream workflow entry files are renamed from SKILL.md to WORKFLOW.md in this Codex package so Codex registers only the root academic-research-suite-ads router skill.
Vague paper-topic requests without a clear research question are routed to deep-research socratic mode before outline, drafting, or full pipeline execution.
docs/superpowers/ is intentionally excluded; it contains upstream Claude Code development plans referencing superpowers:* sub-skills which do not exist in the Codex runtime.
User-facing setup docs include reinstall, /skills verification, and Codex CLI smoke-test guidance for existing-session cache behavior.
Root README.md and SKILL.md define Codex install/runtime behavior; vendored upstream docs remain traceability/reference material unless explicitly adapted here.
The v3.7.1 pattern-protection validator is patched to resolve git object paths from the nested Codex vendored root.

The spec-consistency validator is patched for the Codex distribution: non-vendored .claude/CLAUDE.md is skipped, excluded showcase PDF links are not treated as broken, and WORKFLOW.md replaces internal SKILL.md entry files.
The shared metadata linter auto-detects this Codex distribution and validates vendored WORKFLOW.md entry files instead of upstream SKILL.md files.
The Codex package carries scripts/codex_v3_6_7_block_baseline.json so first-time vendored trees can enforce v3.6.7 block byte-equivalence before Git history contains the upstream manifest.
The Codex audit wrapper is patched to run on macOS stock Bash 3.2+ instead of requiring Bash 4+.
Vendored test utilities keep macOS stock Python 3.9 compatibility where Codex local validation depends on it.

ARS v3.18.0 release content is the tracked upstream content; nested upstream .github workflows, release-discipline tools, and root agents mirrors are preserved for traceability and upstream self-tests but are not repo-level CI or Codex entrypoints. Claude/plugin loader files under .claude, .claude-plugin, and upstream skills aliases remain intentionally excluded.
ARS_MODEL_TIERING is classification-compatible but remains advisory in Codex unless the runtime exposes explicit per-dispatch model selection; unset and unsupported cases keep the active model.
The upstream model-tiering validator excludes the separately vendored experiment-agent tree from its frozen 39-agent ARS classification sweep.
Korean intent triggers are propagated into the root Codex router and optional full-runtime planner because vendored WORKFLOW.md frontmatter is not independently registered.
The live cross-model smoke test remains manual-only and cross-model verification retains the explicit provider, content, credential, and consent gate.

The v3.17 canonical cross-model handoff envelope is preserved; dispatched owners emit a transport request and the dispatching Codex context validates and routes it without widening owner capabilities.
The v3.17 tools allowlist remains a least-privilege role boundary; Codex inline execution does not reinterpret Claude tool names as additional authority.

The degradation registry, executable panel checker, pipeline-boundary lock, tools-allowlist lock, and hermetic transport fixtures are vendored with their upstream tests; the pipeline-orchestrator content-lock hash is updated only for the Codex WORKFLOW.md path overlay.

The v3.18 fixed-seat cross-model Reviewer 2 track and re-review judge pass remain gated by explicit provider configuration and content consent; dispatch failure or single-family execution is disclosed rather than simulated.

The v3.18 citation-cache staleness advisory, opt-in live re-validation, risk-stratified claim gate, scope and novelty advisories, and held-out pipeline-behavior set are preserved with their upstream tests and schemas.

The upstream SessionStart update reminder is vendored for traceability and testing but is not installed or executed by Codex; plugin users update by refreshing the ars-codex-ads marketplace and re-adding the plugin, while direct skill users reinstall manually.

ARS-Codex-ADS Desktop plugin packaging uses a materialized skills directory under plugins/ars-codex/skills instead of a symlink so Windows plugin caches can register the bundled academic-research-suite-ads skill.
Cross-model API behavior is disabled by default and requires explicit user request, provider configuration, and user consent before any external upload.
